key_insight: An autonomous, no-code AI workflow built a calibrated thyroid nodule ultrasound classifier that performed strongly in both internal and external validation.
clinical_relevance: The study suggests that accessible AI tools could help develop reproducible malignancy classifiers for thyroid nodules, including across multiple cancer histologies.
method: Used the no-code Hugging Face ML-Intern agent to autonomously review data, choose/train a model, and calibrate probabilities.
Trained a ResNet-18 on the TN5000 ultrasound dataset (3500 training, 500 validation, 1000 test images).
Externally validated the model on 232 nodules from the University of Colorado, including several thyroid cancer subtypes.
evidence_signal: Performance was strong on internal testing (AUROC 0.94) and remained high on external validation (AUROC 0.90), with sensitivity 0.92 and specificity 0.68.
watch_item: Prospective validation and local recalibration are still needed before clinical use, especially given the moderate external specificity and single-site external cohort.
paper_title: An Agentic, No Code Artificial Intelligence Workflow for Developing and Externally Validating a Thyroid Nodule Ultrasound Malignancy Classifier
